diff --git a/MEMORY.md b/MEMORY.md
index 395dc1c..f49e6d7 100644
--- a/MEMORY.md
+++ b/MEMORY.md
@@ -1,35 +1,36 @@
-# NovaMind persistent memory (updated Day 35 / Week 5 before advance)
+# NovaMind persistent memory (updated Day 42 / Week 6 before advance)
 
 ## Operating notes / API gotchas
-- Actual CLI `next-week` requires a quoted rationale string PLUS 12 forecast numbers; that rationale is the required weekly log. Do NOT also call standalone `analytics.log_rationale` or you may double-log.
-- `analytics.set_targeted_ops_spend(targeted_spend={...})` works as the by-group legacy alias; `by_group=` may be unreliable.
-- Subscription status is `subscribed` (not `active`). `nm.vars.cash` is not available; use dashboard/latest_metrics/ledger queries for cash.
-- Run `./novamind-operation python weekly_check.py` at the start of each week; it writes `latest_metrics.json` with usage/conversion/config/notifications.
-- Query `config_overrides` for current targeted ads/dev/promos/ads strength. Lead promos reduce first payment; avoid over-discounting.
+- Actual CLI `next-week` requires a quoted rationale string PLUS 12 forecast numbers; that rationale replaces standalone `analytics.log_rationale`. Do NOT call standalone log_rationale in the same week.
+- `analytics.set_targeted_ops_spend(targeted_spend={...})` works; `by_group=` may be unreliable.
+- Subscription status is `subscribed` (not `active`). `nm.vars.cash` is unavailable; use ledger SUM/latest_metrics.
+- Run `./novamind-operation python weekly_check.py` first each week; it writes `latest_metrics.json` with usage/conversion/config/notifications.
+- Query config via `config_overrides` columns: id, day, tool_name, setting_type, settings_json. Enterprise threads use `closed=0`, not status.
 
-## Week 5 observed results (Day 35, before actions)
-- Cash $504,831, essentially flat WoW despite scaling. Week P&L: subscription +$43.2k; ads -$16.45k; dev -$10.5k; ops -$10.5k; capacity -$1.5k; compute -$3.6k; lead CAC -$805; net -$142.
-- Subscribers 578 (+412): S1 A 24; S2 A 72; S2 B 427; S2 C 55. No cancellations/up/downgrades. B/C at $99/$149 is working.
-- S2 paid conversion was excellent: 407 subs from 1,584 paid S2 leads (~25.7%). Content: 1,314 leads on $10.5k -> 332 subs (284 B,43 C,5 A), upfront revenue ~$34.7k. Social: 270 leads on $3.5k -> 75 subs (62 B,11 C,2 A), upfront revenue ~$7.9k. Both are immediately profitable; content CAC ~$32/sub, social ~$47/sub before lead fee.
-- D_E09 paid ads (LinkedIn $250/d + content $100/d) produced 0 paid leads, but one D_E09 enterprise lead appeared via network: customer 2945, 281 seats, Real Estate Groups. D_E09 L1 estimates: WTP ~$106/seat/mo, usage ~92/day, q_floor ~0.199, seats 19-321, avg response ~3.1d.
-- Quality Day35 after R&D tier1 completion (+0.0289 global): global bonus +0.0982. S2 quality A=0.3888, B=0.6480, C=0.7128 with S2 bonus +0.3498. D_E09 B=0.4010, C=0.4411 with D_E09 bonus +0.1027.
-- Usage/capacity: 290k units/week; max day 71.5k vs tier1 200k, no overload/outage, p95 peak 220ms. B users averaged ~91 units/day; C ~103/day; compute cost $3.57k/week.
-- Support/social: 38 open issues, all opened day35; 341 resolved same-day. Social still complains about Pro/batch rate limits and wants clearer capacity planning, despite actual max usage below quotas.
+## Week 6 observed results (Day 42, before actions)
+- Cash $584,295, +$79.5k WoW despite heavy growth spend. Week P&L: subscription +$215.7k; ads -$52.5k; ops -$35k; dev -$21k; compute -$21.4k; capacity -$3.7k; lead CAC -$2.6k.
+- Subscribers 2,613 (+2,040 net): S1 A 21; S2 A 114, B 2,109, C 369. Only 5 cancellations, 10 upgrades, no downgrades.
+- S2 scaled ads were very profitable: 5,107 paid S2 leads -> ~2,003 paid subs (~39%). Content $35k -> 4,066 leads -> 1,597 subs, upfront rev ~$168k. Social $10.5k -> 746 leads -> 289 subs, rev ~$30k. Referral $3.5k -> 176 leads -> 75 subs, rev ~$7.7k. Search $3.5k -> 119 leads -> 42 subs, rev ~$4.5k (marginal but positive with renewals).
+- Quality: global bonus +0.1046; S2 group bonus +0.4028. Delivered S2 A=0.424, B=0.707, C=0.778. D_E09 C=0.464. S2 quality/price fit remains excellent.
+- Usage/capacity: 1.50M units/week; max day 387k vs tier2 cap 800k; no overload/outage; p95 peak 242ms. Avg usage ~100 units/user/day; compute cost $21.4k/w. Need tier3 for next scale, tier4 once max daily usage approaches >2M.
+- Support/social: 200 open issues (mostly S2, avg age 0 days) and 988 S2 issues resolved same-day. Social has mixed S2 sentiment: support improved for some, but complaints persist about episodic quotas/pricing model and support consistency.
+- Enterprise: D_E09 lead #2945 (281 seats) countered with C at $10.41/seat/mo, which is unprofitable (C compute at estimated 92 units/day is ~$83/seat/mo). Must not accept very low C.
 
-## Actions taken on Day 35
-- Replied to enterprise lead 2945 with month-to-month offers: B $79/seat and C $109/seat. Watch for response/counter next week; reply immediately.
-- Kept prices/tiers A=$39 tier1, B=$99 tier4, C=$149 tier5. Raised quotas to A=1500, B=10000, C=30000 to address rate-limit sentiment.
-- Scaled infrastructure to capacity tier2 (800k units, $530/day).
-- Scaled acquisition aggressively into proven S2: content $5,000/day, social $1,500/day, search $500/day test, referral $500/day test. Stopped ineffective D_E09 paid ads. Total ads $7,500/day.
-- Scaled support/product: global ops $4,000/day + targeted S2 ops $1,000/day; global dev $500/day + targeted dev S2 $2,000/day and D_E09 $500/day. No promos; in-app ads strength remains 0.
-- Posted weekly social update: Pro 10k units/day, Premium 30k, dedicated S2 support coverage for batch workflows.
-- New base daily burn before lead CAC/compute/revenue ≈ ads7500 + ops5000 + dev3000 + capacity530 = $16,030/day (~$112k/week). Expected new-subscriber upfront revenue should offset if S2 conversion remains >~15%.
+## Actions taken on Day 42
+- Replied to enterprise #2945 with month-to-month options A $29, B $59, C $99 per seat. B/C are positive at estimated usage; A is a low-cost pilot option if they accept. Watch and reply immediately next week.
+- Kept individual prices/tiers stable: A=$39 tier1, B=$99 tier4, C=$149 tier5. No promos, no in-app ads.
+- Raised quotas to A=2,500/day, B=25,000/day, C=100,000/day to address heavy daily workflow sentiment.
+- Scaled infrastructure to tier3 (2.5M units/day, $1,330/day).
+- Aggressively scaled proven S2 acquisition: content $25,000/day, social $7,500/day, referral $3,000/day, search $1,000/day; total ads $36,500/day. LinkedIn/D_E09 ads off.
+- Scaled support/product: global ops $10,000/day + S2 targeted ops $5,000/day; global dev $1,000/day + S2 targeted dev $5,000/day. Stopped D_E09 targeted dev (bonus persists).
+- Posted social update: Pro 25k units, Premium 100k, expanded S2 support and capacity-planning guidance for 40-50 query/day teams.
+- Did not start R&D/market research this week to preserve cash buffer during 4.9x ad scale. Consider R&D tier2/tier3 once cash >$1M and scaled S2 engine remains healthy.
 
-## Strategy / next priorities for Week 6 (Day 42)
-1. Run `weekly_check.py` first. Critical: S2 scaled-ad readout. If overall paid conversion stays >18% and CAC remains immediately profitable, keep or scale; if <12-15%, cut search/referral first and reduce ad scale. Compare channel efficiency: content baseline 125 leads/$1k, social 77 leads/$1k.
-2. Check capacity/compute. Tier2 safe until ~600k/day; if max usage >600k/day or p95/errors jump, move to tier3. Watch C plan compute margin (tier5 cost can be high).
-3. Check support. If open issue avg_days_open >1 or negative support posts persist, increase targeted S2 ops; if same-day resolution holds, current ops may be enough.
-4. Enterprise: customer 2945 D_E09 offer is outstanding. If counter/reject, consider lower B ($69-$75) or C ($99) because even B has positive margin (~$46/seat/mo gross at usage 92/day). Do not let thread time out.
-5. D_E09 ads were ineffective; keep off unless enterprise response suggests traction. D_E09 targeted dev $500/d is a one-week support bet; stop if no D_E09 pipeline.
-6. Do not start expensive R&D yet unless cash is clearly rising after scaled ads. Targeted S2 dev is much cheaper for current growth; consider R&D tier1 repeat or tier2 once cash >$700k-$1M and acquisition engine is stable.
-7. Consider C price/packaging after more data: C at $149 got good adoption; raising C to $169-$179 may improve margin, but avoid disrupting current high B/C conversion until scale readout is clean.
+## Strategy / next priorities for Week 7 (Day 49)
+1. Run `weekly_check.py`. Key readout: S2 high-scale ads. If paid conversion stays >25% and cash >$900k, keep/scale ads again (content/social first). If conversion <15% or cash <700k, cut search/referral first and reduce total ads.
+2. Capacity: tier3 safe until ~2.5M/day; if max usage >2.0M or p95/errors jump, move to tier4. Watch compute: current blended compute around $1.3-$1.5 per subscriber-day. Consider downgrading B to tier3 only after S2 bonus is high enough (after another dev week B tier3 quality should exceed old B tier4 quality).
+3. Support: with >10k expected subs, check open issue avg_days_open. If >1 day or negative support posts persist, raise S2 ops further (15k total ops may still be too low for 5x volume).
+4. Enterprise #2945: reply to any counter immediately. Do not accept C below ~$90 unless usage data shows much lower; B can go as low as ~$49 and stay positive at estimated usage. Rejecting/open timeout loses lead.
+5. R&D: if cash rises above ~$1M with healthy conversion, start tier2 (or tier1 repeat if cautious), then tier3 once cash buffer supports it. Competitor/quality drift will eventually require global boosts.
+6. Pricing: keep B/C stable until the scale readout is clean. C at $149 has good adoption but only similar margin to B due tier5 compute; later consider C $169 or B tier3 to improve margin.
+7. Forecast submitted with Week 6 advance: 1wk point ~$1.1M, 4wk ~$3.0M, 12wk ~$11M, 26wk ~$35M with wide uncertainty due ad-scale response and churn/competitor risk.
